top_tier.name: Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters (KACHQ)
top_tier.notes: Wartime joint operational command coordinating both Artesh and IRGC under the Supreme Leader. Activated in threat scenarios; also oversees IRGC civil-engineering conglomerate of the same name. [DIA IMP 2019 p.17] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: leadership disruptions reported 2025-2026]

top_tier.name: Islamic Republic of Iran Army (Artesh / IRIGF)
top_tier.notes: Regular (conventional) ground force. ~350,000 personnel of which roughly half are conscripts serving 18-24 month terms. Parallels the IRGC GF in mission but answers up a separate chain to AFGS. [DIA IMP 2019 p.17] Commander as of 2019; current commander status [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN].

top_tier.name: Islamic Republic of Iran Air Force (IRIAF)
top_tier.notes: Artesh air component; ~37,000 personnel. Operates mixed Soviet/US/Chinese legacy fleet. Severely degraded by Israeli/US strikes in 2025. Readiness substantially reduced across most TABs. [DIA IMP 2019 p.24] [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: multiple TABs struck June 2025; Su-35E deliveries begun 2025].

top_tier.name: Islamic Republic of Iran Navy (IRIN)
top_tier.notes: Artesh blue-water naval component; ~18,000 personnel. Operates blue-water fleet in Indian Ocean, Persian Gulf, and Caspian. Distinct from IRGCN in mission and doctrine. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27] [ONI Iran Naval Doctrine 2017] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: Bandar Abbas base struck March 2026; Makran set ablaze per satellite imagery].

top_tier.name: IRGC Ground Forces (IRGCGF)
top_tier.notes: IRGC ground component; ~150,000 personnel. 31 provincial corps plus Tehran city corps. Primarily light infantry/commando with independent maneuver brigades. Elite Saberin units. Army Aviation (IRGCAA) formed 2016. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: General Pakpur killed in airstrike per JPost March 2026; successor unknown].

top_tier.name: IRGC Aerospace Force (IRGCASF / IRGC-AF)
top_tier.notes: Holds ALL of Iran's ballistic missiles. NOT the Artesh. ~15,000 personnel. Also operates UAV fleet and some aircraft. Al-Ghadir Missile Command (AGMC) is primary subordinate for missile forces. Renamed from IRGC Air Force to IRGC Aerospace Force 2009. [DIA IMP 2019 p.21] [Iran Watch 2020] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: ISW Oct 2024 assessed all 4 S-300 batteries inoperable; solid-fuel mixing equipment destroyed; Khojir missile production complex struck].

top_tier.name: IRGC Navy (IRGCN)
top_tier.notes: IRGC naval component; ~20,000 personnel. Asymmetric/swarm doctrine in Persian Gulf. Hundreds of small fast-attack craft, missile boats, and coastal ASCMs. Entirely distinct from IRIN. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: Bandar Abbas 1st IRGCN district and Bandar Mahshahr 3rd district struck March 2026 per Alma Research; at least 5 vessels hit/sunk at Bandar Abbas per MAIAR; 3 of 5 HQs targeted].

top_tier.name: IRGC Quds Force (IRGC-QF)
top_tier.hq: Tehran (former US Embassy compound and IRGC HQ)
top_tier.notes: External operations branch of IRGC. ~5,000-21,000 personnel (estimates vary widely). Qaani replaced Soleimani on 3 Jan 2020 following US drone strike assassination. Operates via geographic corps/desks. [DIA IMP 2019 p.22] [Wikipedia Quds Force] [CTP 2025] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN].

top_tier.name: Basij Resistance Force
top_tier.notes: IRGC paramilitary/mobilization force. Active reserve ~450,000; inactive reserve up to 1 million; total nominal strength 4-5 million with basic training. 32 provincial commands (mirrors IRGC corps + Tehran). Core unit types: Ashura/Al Zahra (local), Beyt ol Moghaddas/Kowsar (rapid reaction), Imam Ali (internal), Imam Hossein (light infantry), Fatehin (SF). [DIA IMP 2019 p.19] [CTP Mar 2026].

second_tier.name: Northwestern Regional Headquarters
second_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Amir Hossein Shafiei (since Jul 2024). Covers Ardabil, East Azerbaijan, Ghazvin, Hamedan, West Azerbaijan, Zanjan provinces. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

second_tier.name: Western Regional Headquarters
second_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Soleiman Taheri (since May 2021). Covers Ilam, Kermanshah, Kurdistan provinces. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

second_tier.name: Southwestern Regional Headquarters
second_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Hossein Mohammad Shafiei (since Jan 2025). Covers Khuzestan, Lorestan. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

second_tier.name: Northeastern Regional Headquarters
second_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Cyrus Amanollahi (since Jul 2024). Covers Golestan, Khorasan Razavi, Mazandaran, North Khorasan, Semnan, South Khorasan. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

second_tier.name: Southeastern Regional Headquarters
second_tier.notes: Commander: BG Amir Gholam Alian (since Aug 2020). Covers Hormozgan, Kerman, Sistan and Baluchistan, parts of Yazd. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

second_tier.name: 1st Tactical Air Base — Mehrabad (Tehran)
second_tier.notes: MiG-29A/UB, transports, VIP fleet, tankers, C-130, CH-47C. Attacked by Israeli Air Force June 13, 2025; airworthiness doubtful. Hosts IACI and IHSMC maintenance facilities. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: 2nd Tactical Air Base — Tabriz (Shaheed Fakouri)
second_tier.notes: F-5E/F, MiG-29A/UB, Saeghe variants. Attacked Jun 13, 2025. Northern Helicopter Command HQ and UAV Logistics Support Command co-located. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: 3rd Tactical Air Base — Hamadan (Nojeh)
second_tier.notes: F-4E, RF-4E. Su-35E ordered (o/o 2025). Attacked Jun 13, 2025. Underground tunnel expansion reported. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [DIA IMP 2019 p.24] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: 4th Tactical Air Base — Dezful (Vahdati)
second_tier.notes: F-5E/F. Attacked Jun 13, 2025; airworthiness doubtful. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: 5th Tactical Air Base — Omidiyeh (Shahid Ardestani)
second_tier.notes: F-7N/FT-7N (3 squadrons). Was due to transfer to IRGC in 2008; reverted to IRIAF. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [DIA IMP 2019 p.24]

second_tier.name: 6th Tactical Air Base — Bushehr (Yassini)
second_tier.notes: F-4E; Su-35E on order 2025. Attacked Jun 13 and Jun 19, 2025. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: 7th Tactical Air Base — Shiraz (Shahid Dastghaib)
second_tier.notes: Su-24MK (3 sqns), C-130 airlift wing, Il-76TD, P-3F ASW. Attacked Jun 13, 2025. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [DIA IMP 2019 p.24] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: 8th Tactical Air Base — Isfahan (Shahid Beheshti)
second_tier.notes: F-14A/AM (3 sqns). Under heavy attack Mar 2026; IDF reportedly destroyed entire F-14 fleet plus Su-22s, P-3s, C-130s. Also base for Yak-130 trainers (2 delivered Sep 2023) and PC-7/S-68 training. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: F-14 fleet assessed destroyed]

second_tier.name: 9th Tactical Air Base — Bandar Abbas
second_tier.notes: F-4E; Su-35E on order. Also operates dispersed underground base Oghab 44 in Zagros Mountains (unveiled Jan 2023). [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: 10th Tactical Air Base — Chahbahar (Konarak)
second_tier.notes: F-4D, Mirage F-1EQ/BQ (ex-Iraqi). Mirages relocated from TAB14 2011-2012. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [DIA IMP 2019 p.24]

second_tier.name: 14th Tactical Air Base — Mashhad (Shahid Hashemi Nejad)
second_tier.notes: F-5E/F. Eastern Area Command main repair facilities. Attacked Jun 15, 2025; some transports/tankers destroyed. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: IRIN 1st Naval District — Bandar Abbas (SNFHQ)
second_tier.notes: Southern Naval Force HQ (SNFHQ); RA Abrahim Ashkan commanded deployed naval groups (2017). Strait of Hormuz. Kilo-class submarines based here. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27] [ONI Iran Naval Doctrine 2017] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: base struck Mar 2026; Makran set ablaze; multiple warships damaged]

second_tier.name: IRIN 2nd Naval District — Bushehr/Jask
second_tier.notes: Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman. Jask base increasingly important for Indian Ocean force projection. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27]

second_tier.name: IRIN 3rd Naval District — Chah Bahar
second_tier.notes: Gulf of Oman operations; entry/exit point for Indian Ocean deployments. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27] [ONI Iran Naval Doctrine 2017]

second_tier.name: IRIN 4th Naval District — Bandar Anzali (Caspian)
second_tier.notes: Caspian Sea flotilla. Separate threat environment from southern naval forces. Operates Bayandor-class corvettes and patrol craft on Caspian. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27]

second_tier.name: IRIN 5th Naval District — Jask (Indian Ocean)
second_tier.notes: Forward operating base for extended IO deployments. Supports IRIN blue-water presence. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27; note: some sources fold Jask into 2nd ND; listed separately here per IRIN reorganization reporting]

second_tier.name: IRGCN 1st Naval Region — Bandar Abbas (Saheb al-Zaman)
second_tier.notes: Capt. Abbas Gholamshahi (per Wikipedia IRGCN). Strait of Hormuz. Largest IRGCN base. Struck Feb 28 - Mar 7, 2026; multiple drydocks hit, vessels neutralized per Alma Research/satellite imagery. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [Alma Research Mar 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: IRGCN 2nd Naval Region — Bushehr
second_tier.notes: North-central Persian Gulf. Fast-attack craft and missile boat operations. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28]

second_tier.name: IRGCN 3rd Naval Region — Bandar Mahshahr
second_tier.hq: Bandar Mahshahr (Bandar Imam Khomeini area), Khuzestan
second_tier.notes: Northern Persian Gulf. Struck by at least 6 munitions Mar 2, 2026 per Alma Research satellite imagery. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [Alma Research Mar 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: IRGCN 4th Naval Region — Asaluyeh
second_tier.notes: Central Persian Gulf; guards South Pars gas field approaches. Not targeted as of Mar 2026 per Alma Research. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [IFMAT 2017]

second_tier.name: IRGCN 5th Naval Region — Bandar Lengeh (Imam Mohammad Bagher)
second_tier.notes: Southern Persian Gulf; controls access to disputed islands (Lesser Tunb, Greater Tunb, Abu Musa). Not targeted as of Mar 2026 per Alma Research. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [Wikipedia IRGCN]

second_tier.name: Al-Ghadir Missile Command (AGMC)
second_tier.notes: Primary ballistic missile operational command under IRGC-AF. Commander: BG Mahmoud Bagheri Kazemabad (per Iran Watch 2020). Coordinates all SRBM/MRBM/IRBM brigades. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020] [DIA IMP 2019 p.21] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: solid-fuel mixing equipment destroyed Oct 2024; Khojir complex struck; production degraded but mobile launchers unaffected]

second_tier.name: IRGC Aerospace Force Space Command
second_tier.notes: Manages IRGC satellite program. Launched Nour-1 (Apr 2020), Nour-2 (Mar 2022), Nour-3 (Sep 2023) via Qased SLV from Shahroud. [Wikipedia IRGCASF] [Iran Watch 2020]

second_tier.name: IRGC Aerospace Force UAV Command
second_tier.notes: Primary operator of Iran's Shahed-129/136, Mohajer-6, Ababil, Fotros, Karrar UAV families. Supplies Shahed-136 to Russia for Ukraine war. Technical support from Rayan Roshd Afzar. [DIA IMP 2019 p.21] [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020]

second_tier.name: IRGC Aerospace Force Air Defense Component
second_tier.notes: Operates Raad/3rd Khordad, Talash/15th Khordad, Misagh MANPADS. Works in tandem with IRIADF (Artesh) under KADHQ. All 4 S-300 PMU2 batteries assessed inoperable after Apr and Oct 2024 Israeli strikes. [ISW Oct 2024 PDF] [DIA IMP 2019 p.22] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: Quds Force Ramadan Headquarters (Iraq Corps)
second_tier.notes: 1st Corps; responsible for Iraq. Formerly led by BG Hassan Danaeifar and BG Iraj Masjedi. Coordinates with PMF (Popular Mobilization Forces) and Iran-aligned militias. [Wikipedia Quds Force]

second_tier.name: Quds Force Lebanon/Syria Corps (7th Corps)
second_tier.notes: Responsible for Lebanon (Hezbollah), Syria. Formerly led by BG Hossein Dehghan, BG Mohammad Hejazi, BG Mohammad Reza Zahedi (Zahedi killed Israeli strike Damascus Apr 2024). Unit 2250 (logistical) coordinated Syrian support. [Wikipedia Quds Force] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: Zahedi killed Apr 2024]

second_tier.name: Quds Force Arabian Peninsula Corps (6th Corps)
second_tier.notes: Responsible for Persian Gulf countries, Arabian Peninsula, Yemen Houthi (Ansar Allah) support. Led by BG Abdolreza Shahlaei (responsible for arms/training to Houthis). [Wikipedia Quds Force] [WINEP Knights]

second_tier.name: Quds Force Afghanistan/Pakistan Corps (4th Corps / Al-Ansar)
second_tier.notes: Al-Ansar Command Center; responsible for Afghanistan and Central Asia. Led formerly by General Hossein Musavi and Colonel Hasan Mortezavi. Recruits Fatemiyoun Afghan Shia fighters. [Wikipedia Quds Force]

second_tier.name: Quds Force Palestine Division
second_tier.notes: Led by BG Saeed Izadi. Responsible for Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad support. Includes Abu Jihad Foreign Operations Unit (Gaza tunnels, IED networks). [Wikipedia Quds Force]

second_tier.name: Sarallah Operational Headquarters (Tehran region)
second_tier.notes: Multi-provincial HQ overseeing Tehran region IRGC GF, including Mohammad Rasoul Allah Provincial Corps (Tehran City) and Imam Hassan Mojtaba Corps (Alborz). Oversees 22 Basij regional bases in Tehran City. Sarallah HQ struck by IDF Mar 1, 2026. [CTP Mar 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

second_tier.name: Hamze-ye Sayyed al-Shohada Operational HQ (West/NW)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Ashura (E. Azerbaijan), Shohada (W. Azerbaijan), Kurdistan Beit-ol-Moqaddas corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Najaf-e Ashraf Operational HQ (West/Central)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Amir al-Mouminin (Ilam), Kermanshah Nebi Akram, Ansar al-Hossein (Hamedan) corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Karbala Operational HQ (South)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Vali-ye Asr (Khuzestan), Abolfazl (Lorestan), Fath (Kohgiluyeh) corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Madineh-ye al-Munavareh Operational HQ (South-Southwest)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Fajr (Fars), Imam Sadeq (Bushehr), Imam Sadjad (Hormozgan) corps. Most important units include Fajr Division Shiraz and 33rd SF Brigade. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Ghadir Operational HQ (North/Caspian)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Qods (Gilan), Karbala (Mazandaran), Neynava (Golestan) corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Samen al-Aeme Operational HQ (Northeast)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Imam Reza (Razavi Khorasan), Javad al-Aeme (N. Khorasan), Ansar al-Reza (S. Khorasan) corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Saheb al-Zaman Operational HQ (Central)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Ruhollah (Markazi), Saheb al-Amr (Qazvin), Ali bin Abu Taleb (Qom), Ghaem al-Muhammad (Semnan) corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Sayyed al-Shohada Operational HQ (Central)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Ghamar Bani Hashem (Chaharmahal), Saheb al-Zaman Isfahan (Isfahan), al-Ghadir (Yazd) corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Qods Operational HQ (Southeast)
second_tier.notes: Oversees Sarallah (Kerman), Salman (Sistan and Baluchestan) corps. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

second_tier.name: Ashura Operational HQ (Northwest — East/West Azarbaijan, Ardabil, Zanjan)
second_tier.notes: Commander: BG Mohammad Taghi Osanlou (holds command of Ashura and Hamze). Oversees Ashura (E. Azerbaijan), Hazrat-e Abbas (Ardabil), Ansar al-Mahdi (Zanjan). [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: 16th Armored Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Ali Karimi (since Feb 2025). Subordinate brigades: 116th Mechanized Assault Bde (Ghazvin), 316th Armored Bde (Hamedan). Post-2008 reorganization variant of older 16th Armored Division. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 21st Infantry Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Issa Bayat (since Sep 2024). Subordinate brigades: 121st Bde (Tabriz), 221st Bde (Maragheh), 321st Mobile Assault Bde (Marand). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 64th Infantry Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Abbas Jamshidi (since Jan 2024). Three mobile assault subordinate brigades: 164th (Piranshahr), 264th (Salmas), 364th (Mahabad). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 81st Armored Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Jalal Salehi (since Oct 2022). Brigades: 181st Armored (Eslamabad-e Gharb), 281st Armored (Bisotun), 71st Mechanized Infantry (Sarpol-e Zahab). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 28th Infantry Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Jaber Abad Zordashti (since Dec 2024). Brigades: 128th MAB (Sanandaj), 228th MAB (Saghez), 328th MAB (Marivan). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 92nd Armored Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Mehrdad Amjadian (since Jan 2023). Iran's premier armored formation in southwest; guards Khuzestan oil fields. Brigades: 192nd Armored (Ahvaz), 292nd Armored (Dezful), 392nd Armored (Dasht-e Azadegan), 72nd Bde (Khorramshahr). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 84th Infantry Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Bakhtiyar Karaminia (since Aug 2024). Brigades: 184th MAB (Khorramabad). Three deaths reported after Israeli drone strike June 19, 2025. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 77th Infantry Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Abdol Rahman Sasanian Fard. Brigades: 277th MAB (Ghuchan), 377th MAB (Mashhad), 38th Armored (Torbat-e Jam). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 30th Infantry Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: Unknown. Brigades: 130th Bde (Bojnourd). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 58th Mobile Assault Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Bahadar Khajehvand (since Apr 2024). Brigades: 158th MAB (Shahroud), 258th MAB (Chehel Dokhtar). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 88th Armored Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Adel Heydari (since Oct 2023). Border-focused; SE Iran vs. Pakistan/Afghanistan threat axes. Brigades: 188th Armored (Zahedan), 288th Armored (Khash), 388th Mechanized Assault (Iranshahr). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 23rd Mobile Assault Division
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Akbar Najafi. Central reserve division; not under any regional HQ. Brigades: 123rd MAB, 223rd SF Bde. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 65th Airborne Special Forces Brigade (NOHED)
third_tier.notes: Iran's most capable special forces unit; jump-qualified. Commander: Col. Asghar Rafiei (since Nov 2024). Directly under IRIGF HQ. NOT part of any regional command. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18] [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 55th Airborne Brigade
third_tier.notes: Commander: Col. Farhang Dehghani (since Dec 2024). One of two Artesh airborne brigades; complements NOHED. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025] [DIA IMP 2019]

third_tier.name: 25th Special Forces Brigade
third_tier.notes: Commander: Col. Hossein Sadegh Zadeh (since Jan 2025). Special Forces; part of Northwest command. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 35th Special Forces Brigade
third_tier.notes: Commander: Unknown. Part of Western command. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 45th Special Forces Brigade
third_tier.notes: Commander: BG2 Mohammad Ebrahimi (since Dec 2022). [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 37th Armored Brigade
third_tier.notes: Commander: Col. Karam Ghorbani (since May 2025). Independent brigade in Fars; not under regional HQ. [CTP Artesh OOB Aug 2025]

third_tier.name: 11th Tactical Fighter Squadron (MiG-29)
third_tier.notes: MiG-29A/UB Fulcrum air superiority. Post-June 2025 strikes operational status uncertain. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 21st Tactical Fighter Squadron (F-5E)
third_tier.notes: F-5E/F Tiger II. Some transferred to TAB14/Mashhad. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 22nd Tactical Fighter Squadron (MiG-29)
third_tier.notes: MiG-29A (8) MiG-29UB (3); air superiority in NW Iran. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 23rd Tactical Fighter Squadron (Saeghe/F-5 variant)
third_tier.notes: F-5E Saeghe and Saeghe II (indigenous F-5 variant). [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026]

third_tier.name: 31st Tactical Fighter Squadron (F-4E)
third_tier.notes: F-4E Phantom II. Su-35E ordered (o/o 2025). TAB 3 struck Jun 13, 2025. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 31st Tactical Reconnaissance Squadron (RF-4E)
third_tier.notes: RF-4E Phantom II reconnaissance variant; Iran's only dedicated tactical recon aircraft. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 41st Tactical Fighter Squadron (F-5E/F)
third_tier.notes: F-5E/F Tiger II. TAB 4 struck Jun 13, 2025. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 51st/52nd/53rd Tactical Fighter Squadrons (F-7N)
third_tier.notes: Three squadrons of F-7N/FT-7N (license-built MiG-21). [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026]

third_tier.name: 61st Tactical Fighter Squadron (F-4E)
third_tier.notes: F-4E Phantom II; Su-35E on order. TAB 6 attacked Jun 13 and Jun 19, 2025. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 71st/72nd/73rd Tactical Fighter Squadrons (Su-24MK)
third_tier.notes: Three squadrons of Su-24MK Fencer interdiction/strike aircraft (ex-Iraqi/Russian). TAB 7 struck Jun 13, 2025. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [DIA IMP 2019 p.24] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 71st Anti-Submarine Warfare Squadron (P-3F)
third_tier.notes: P-3F Orion maritime patrol/ASW. Struck Mar 2026 at Isfahan (some P-3s also based/maintained at Isfahan per Scramble). [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: 81st/82nd/83rd Tactical Fighter Squadrons (F-14A)
third_tier.notes: Three squadrons of F-14A/AM Tomcat — Iran's longest-range air superiority fighter. IDF reportedly destroyed the entire F-14 fleet in March 2026 attacks on Isfahan. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN: assessed destroyed Mar 2026]

third_tier.name: 91st Tactical Fighter Squadron (F-4E)
third_tier.notes: F-4E Phantom II; Su-35E on order 2025. Also operates dispersal Oghab 44 underground base in Zagros (unveiled Jan 2023). [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026]

third_tier.name: 101st/102nd Tactical Fighter Squadrons (F-4D / Mirage F-1)
third_tier.notes: F-4D Phantom II (101st TFS) and Mirage F-1EQ/BQ (102nd TFS; ex-Iraqi, relocated from TAB14 2011-12). [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026]

third_tier.name: 141st Tactical Fighter Squadron (F-5E/F)
third_tier.notes: F-5E/F Tiger II. TAB 14 struck Jun 15, 2025; some transports and tankers destroyed. [Scramble.nl IRIAF OOB Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: IRIN Submarine Force (Kilo-class / Fateh-class)
third_tier.notes: 3x Kilo-class (877EKM) attack submarines + 1x Fateh-class coastal submarine. Kilo-class range extends to Indian Ocean. Bandar Abbas base struck Mar 2026. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: IRIN Midget Submarine Force (Ghadir/Nahang/Yono)
third_tier.notes: 14x Ghadir/Yono-class + 1x Nahang-class midget submarines (coastal/littoral). Used for special operations insertion and mine-laying. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27]

third_tier.name: IRIN Surface Combatant Force (Jamaran/Vosper/Bayandor corvettes)
third_tier.notes: 3x Jamaran-class (Mowj) corvette, 3x Vosper Mk 5 corvette, 2x Bayandor (PF-103) corvette. Makran forward base ship set ablaze Mar 2026. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27] [TWZ Mar 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: IRIN Fast Attack Craft Force (Kaman/Combattante II)
third_tier.notes: 13x Kaman-class (Combattante II) FACM + other patrol craft. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27]

third_tier.name: IRGCN Fast-Attack Craft Force (Peykaap / Houdong / C-14)
third_tier.notes: Hundreds of small FAC, speedboats, patrol craft: Houdong (Thondor) x10 FACM, Peykaap I/II/III x45+, C-14 x5, Boghammar x20, many more. Swarm tactics in Hormuz Strait. Base struck Mar 2026. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: IRGCN Sepah Navy Special Force (SNSF) — Forur Island
third_tier.notes: Combat diving, direct action, counterterrorism, VBSS, amphibious assault. Analogous to MARSOF. Forur Island forward base in central Gulf. [DIA IMP 2019 p.29]

third_tier.name: 5th Ra'ad Missile Brigade
third_tier.notes: SRBM brigade; Shahab-1/2, Qiam family. Subordinate to Al-Ghadir Missile Command. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020]

third_tier.name: 7th al-Hadid Missile Brigade
third_tier.notes: MRBM brigade; likely Shahab-3/Ghadr-110/Emad family. Subordinate to AGMC. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020]

third_tier.name: 15th Ghaem Missile Brigade
third_tier.notes: Missile brigade; likely solid-fuel SRBM family (Fateh-110/313, Zolfaghar, Dezful). Subordinate to AGMC. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020]

third_tier.name: 19th Zulfiqar Missile Brigade
third_tier.notes: Likely Zolfaghar (750km solid fuel) and Fateh-313 SRBM. Subordinate to AGMC. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020]

third_tier.name: 23rd Towhid Missile Brigade
third_tier.notes: Missile brigade type unconfirmed in open source. Subordinate to AGMC. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020]

third_tier.name: IRGC-AF Cruise Missile Command (Hoveyzeh/Soumar/Ya-Ali)
third_tier.notes: Operates Hoveyzeh (1,350km cruise), Soumar/Ya-Ali/Paveh land-attack cruise missiles. Received Hoveyzeh delivery 2019 per Iran Watch. Brigade-level organizational structure exists but open-source numbering unconfirmed. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020] [Wikipedia IRGCASF]

third_tier.name: Muhammad Rasoul Allah Provincial Corps (Tehran City)
third_tier.notes: Largest IRGC provincial command. Commander: Mohammadreza Yazdi. Oversees 22+ Basij regional bases in Tehran City. Deployed elements to Syria during civil war. Sarallah HQ struck IDF Mar 1, 2026. [Wikipedia IRGC GF] [CTP Mar 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ashura (East Azerbaijan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Abedin Khorram. Includes cyberspace headquarters. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Shohada (West Azerbaijan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Habib Shahsavari. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Kurdistan Beit-ol-Moqaddas Corps
third_tier.notes: Commander: Sadegh Hosseini. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Hazrat-e Abbas (Ardabil Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Jalil Babazadeh. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ansar al-Mahdi (Zanjan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Jahanbakhsh Karami. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ansar al-Hossein (Hamedan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Mazahar Majidi. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Amir al-Mouminin (Ilam Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Jamal Shakarami. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Kermanshah Nebi Akram Corps
third_tier.notes: Commander: Bahman Reyhani. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ruhollah (Markazi Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Mohsen Karimi. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Saheb al-Amr (Qazvin Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Mohammad Shahrokhi. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ali bin Abu Taleb (Qom Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Nohammad Taghi Shahgheraghi. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ghaem al-Muhammad (Semnan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Hamid Damghani. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Qods (Gilan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Mohammad Abdollahpour. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Neynava (Golestan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Ali Malek Shahkoui. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Karbala (Mazandaran Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Mohammad Hossein Babaei. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Vali-ye Asr (Khuzestan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Hassan Shahvarpour. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Fath (Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Hamid Khoramdel. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Abolfazl (Lorestan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Morteza Kashkouli. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ghamar Bani Hashem (Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Ali Mohammad Akbari. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Saheb al-Zaman (Isfahan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Hossein Fada. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e al-Ghadir (Yazd Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Reza Shamsipour. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Imam Reza (Razavi Khorasan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: YaghoubAli Nazari. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Javad al-Aeme (North Khorasan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Aboulghasem Chaman. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Ansar al-Reza (South Khorasan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Ali Ghasemi. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Imam Sadeq (Bushehr Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Ali Razmjou. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Fajr (Fars Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Hashem Ghiasi. Includes Fajr Division and 33rd Al Mahdi Airborne SF Brigade. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Imam Sadjad (Hormozgan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Abazar Salari. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Sarallah (Kerman Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Hossein Maroufi. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Salman (Sistan and Baluchestan Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Amanollah Garshasbi. Counter-Baluch separatist/Jaish al-Adl focus. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Sepah-e Imam Hassan Mojtaba (Alborz Provincial Corps)
third_tier.notes: Commander: Yousef Molaei. Part of Sarallah HQ which oversees capital region. [Wikipedia IRGC GF]

third_tier.name: Saberin Special Forces Brigade
third_tier.notes: IRGC-GF elite SF; equivalent to Iranian Delta Force. Provincial detachments exist. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]

third_tier.name: 110th Salman Farsi Commando Brigade
third_tier.notes: IRGC-GF commando brigade. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]

third_tier.name: 33rd Al Mahdi Airborne Special Forces Brigade
third_tier.notes: Transitioned from airborne to SF role; under Madineh-ye al-Munavareh HQ. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: T-72S, T-72Z (local variant), Zulfiqar-1/3, Karrar, M60A1, Chieftain Mk 3/5
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED total fleet across Artesh. Most capable: T-72S (~480) and Zulfiqar (~100 domestic). Many Chieftains/M60s in storage or reduced readiness. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]
ESTIMATED. BMP-1/2 (~700 active), M113 (~400 active), remainder various Soviet/US-era APCs. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: M109 Paladin, Type 83 (2S1 variant), Raad-1, Hm-41
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED. Mix of US-origin M109 and domestic derivatives. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]
ESTIMATED total fleet; significant portion in storage. [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: AH-1J Cobra, Bell 214, CH-47C Chinook, AB-206
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED ~300 total; AH-1J Cobras are primary attack helos (~50 operational). [DIA IMP 2019 p.18]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: F-14A/AM Tomcat, F-4D/E Phantom II, F-5E/F Tiger II, MiG-29A/UB, Su-24MK Fencer, Su-22 Fitter, Mirage F-1EQ/BQ, F-7N/FT-7N, Saeghe I/II, Azarakhsh, Kowsar
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED ~350 total nominal; actual operational approx 150-200 pre-2025 strikes. Post Jun/Mar 2025-2026 strikes: F-14 fleet assessed destroyed (Scramble.nl Mar 2026); additional heavy losses across multiple TABs. Su-35E deliveries begun 2025 (numbers unknown). [DIA IMP 2019 p.24] [Scramble.nl Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: C-130E/H Hercules, Il-76TD, B707, B747, F27-400M/600
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED. Some transport/tanker aircraft destroyed at Mashhad (Jun 2025) and Isfahan (Mar 2026). [Scramble.nl Feb 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: Shahab-1 (Scud-B), Shahab-2 (Scud-C), Qiam-1, Fateh-110, Fateh-313, Raad-500, Zolfaghar, Dezful, Haj Qasem, Kheibar Shekan, Fattah-1, Fattah-2
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED 2,000-3,000 SRBMs/MRBMs total (per NTI/Iran Watch); includes all families. ~500+ used in True Promise I/II/III/IV operations 2024-2026. Production degraded by Oct 2024 strikes on solid-fuel mixer (ISW). Iran Watch Jan 2026 estimates 3,000+ ballistic missiles total. [Iran Watch 2026] [ISW Oct 2024] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

ESTIMATED; true counts classified. Jane's (2006) identified 6 Shahab-3 brigades. Sejjil first used operationally in True Promise 4 (Mar 2026). Khorramshahr-4 reportedly used Oct 2024. [Wikipedia IRGCASF] [Iran Watch 2026] [Kurdistan24 Mar 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: Hoveyzeh, Soumar, Ya-Ali, Paveh, Shahab-3D cruise variant
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED. Land-attack cruise missiles with ranges 700-1350km. Hoveyzeh delivery confirmed 2019. [Iran Watch IRGC-AF 2020]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: Shahed-129, Shahed-136 (Geran-2), Mohajer-6, Ababil-2/3, Qasef-1/K2, Fotros, Karrar
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED. Shahed-136 supplied to Russia at scale since 2022. Mohajer-6 carries PGMs. [DIA IMP 2019 p.21] [Iran Watch 2020]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: Bavar-373, S-300PMU2 (4 batteries — ALL ASSESSED INOPERABLE), Raad/3rd Khordad, Talash/15th Khordad, Tor-M1, Misagh-1/2 MANPADS
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED systems/batteries. All 4 S-300 PMU2 batteries assessed inoperable following Apr and Oct 2024 Israeli strikes (destroyed target engagement radars). Bavar-373 (domestic S-300 equivalent) operational status uncertain. Ghadir passive radar x2 struck Oct 2024. [ISW Oct 2024 PDF] [DIA IMP 2019 p.22] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]
ESTIMATED; 3+ assessed hit/sunk at Bandar Abbas Mar 2026 per MAIAR. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: Peykaap I/II/III patrol craft (torpedo/missile), C-14, Boghammar
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED total small-craft fleet (hundreds; counts uncertain). 6 vessels damaged at Konarak per MAIAR Mar 2026. [DIA IMP 2019 p.28] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]

unit_equipment.equipment_model: Kilo-class (877EKM), Fateh-class coastal sub, Ghadir/Yono midget sub, Nahang midget sub
unit_equipment.notes: ESTIMATED: 3 Kilo + 1 Fateh + 14 Ghadir + 1 Nahang = 19 total. Post-Mar 2026 base strikes status uncertain. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]
ESTIMATED: 3 Jamaran + 3 Vosper + 2 Bayandor. Makran (forward base ship) ablaze Mar 2026. [DIA IMP 2019 p.27] [TWZ Mar 2026] [POST-2024-STRIKES STATUS UNCERTAIN]
